hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W/F/Y) will enhance complementarity with SSTR2-specific ECL2 (res 192/193/195/197) and TM5 (205/208/209/212) contacts, while mutating position 11 to hydrophobic residues (L/I/V) will improve steric complementarity in the SSTR2-specific ECL3/TM6 pocket, thereby increasing selectivity margin over off-targets.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current hypothesis remains valid: SSTR2 selectivity can be achieved by optimizing non-pharmacophore positions to disrupt off-target interactions. However, the focus must shift to more aggressive mutation of positions 1, 2, 5, 6, 11, and 12 to push Δmargin further above 0.

proposed_changes.rationale: Targeting non-pharmacophore positions 1, 2, 5, 6, 11, and 12 will allow us to disrupt conserved-pocket contacts with SSTR3/SSTR5 while preserving the pharmacophore FWKT(7-10) and Cys3/Cys14.

These mutations are designed to disrupt conserved interactions at SSTR3/SSTR5 while maintaining the native pharmacophore and disulfide bridge. For example, A1K and A2R can reduce off-target electrostatic interactions, while F5Y and F6W may alter hydrophobic contacts.
